---
title: "Related Research"
format: html
page-layout: full
toc: true
include-in-header:
  - file: header.html
---

::: {.research-card}
## Helping litigants make informed choices in resolving debt disputes 
**Authors:** Pavithra Manivannan, Susan Thomas, and Bhargavi Zaveri-Shah 

**Published:** Leap Blog, June 2023

This research explores how data can empower litigants to make better-informed decisions when facing debt disputes, examining the trade-offs between litigation and settlement.

[Read full article →](https://blog.theleapjournal.org/2023/06/helping-litigants-make-informed-choices.html#gsc.tab=0)
:::

::: {.research-card}
## Evaluating courts from a litigant's perspective: A project report
**Authors:** Pavithra Manivannan, Geetika Palta, Susan Thomas, and Bhargavi Zaveri-Shah  

**Published:** December 2023

A comprehensive project report analyzing court performance from the perspective of litigants, providing insights into case duration, disposal rates, and litigation outcomes.

[Read full report →](https://www.xkdr.org/paper/evaluating-courts-from-a-litigants-perspective-a-project-report)
:::

::: {.research-card}
## Do court vacations matter: evidence from the Bombay High Court
**Authors:** Tushar Anand, Pavithra Manivannan, and Bhargavi Zaveri-Shah 

**Published:** September 2024

This research examines whether court vacations actually contribute to case delays in the Indian judiciary, using evidence from the Bombay High Court to challenge common assumptions about their impact on judicial efficiency. 

[Read full article →](https://blog.theleapjournal.org/2024/09/do-court-vacations-matter-evidence-from.html#gsc.tab=0)
:::
